mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are positive in >60% of observations when FEDFUNDS rises.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate above 4.5 percent signals labor market deterioration and elevated recession probability, triggering negative equity returns as investors price in earnings declines.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in >65% of instances when UNRATE crosses the 4.5 percent threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a broad contraction in consumer demand that reduces corporate revenue expectations, driving negative forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Positive quarterly returns dominate when both RSAFS and HOUST register simultaneous month-over-month declines.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth exceeding two percent annualized signals strong supply-side momentum that supports short-term equity rallies during periods of monetary tightening.
failure_condition: Short-term returns are negative in >60% of observations when INDPRO growth exceeds two percent annualized during tightening phases.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation outpaces the federal funds rate, real interest rates turn negative, prompting equity investors to demand higher risk premiums and generating negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in >60% of periods when CPIAUCSL growth exceeds the federal funds rate.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls indicate expanding economic activity and stronger corporate earnings potential, generating positive forward equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Negative returns follow payroll increases in >60% of observations, or Rank IC falls below 0.03 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth falling below one point five percent annualized signals economic stagnation that reduces corporate profit expectations, causing negative forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Positive quarterly returns dominate when GDPC1 annualized growth drops below one point five percent.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence where industrial production rises while total payrolls fall indicates capital substitution and productivity gains, supporting short-term equity outperformance.
failure_condition: Short-term returns are negative in >60% of observations during periods of positive INDPRO growth combined with negative PAYEMS growth.
